## Current R8/R9 Update - WebSocket OneBot API Timeout Cleanup and Diagnostic Coverage - 2026-07-07 13:41 CST

### Scope

Strengthened deterministic evidence for WebSocket OneBot send API timeout behavior. Previous slices covered malformed WebSocket send responses, structured API-error diagnostics, and pending request cleanup on explicit error responses; this slice proves an unanswered WebSocket API request times out with bounded diagnostics and clears `pendingWsRequests`.

This is coverage hardening only; it does not change runtime behavior, does not run real SnowLuma/QQ acceptance, and does not claim production readiness.

### Commands run in this checkpoint

```bash
date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z %z'
git status --short
pnpm list @earendil-works/pi-agent-core @earendil-works/pi-ai --depth 0
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test:run && git diff --check
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts -t "timed-out WebSocket OneBot API" --silent
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts --silent
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts tests/integration/e2e-conversation.test.ts --silent
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test:run && git diff --check
```

### Results

- R0 drift check at `2026-07-07 13:36:32 CST +0800`: worktree remains highly dirty; Pi packages resolve as `@earendil-works/pi-agent-core 0.80.2` and `@earendil-works/pi-ai 0.80.2`.
- Baseline full deterministic gate before this slice passed: `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test:run && git diff --check` exited `0`; `pnpm test:run` reported `71 passed | 1 skipped` test files and `1037 passed | 22 skipped` tests; `git diff --check` produced no whitespace errors.

- Related OneBot adapter coverage passed: `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts --silent` passed with `42 passed`.
- Related gateway/e2e coverage passed: `pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts tests/integration/e2e-conversation.test.ts --silent` passed with `2 passed` test files and `125 passed` tests.
- Final full deterministic gate passed: `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test:run && git diff --check` exited `0`; `pnpm test:run` reported `71 passed | 1 skipped` test files and `1038 passed | 22 skipped` tests; `git diff --check` produced no whitespace errors.

### Implementation evidence

Changed path in this slice:

- `tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts`
  - adds `cleans up timed-out WebSocket OneBot API requests with bounded diagnostics`.
  - uses fake timers to advance the existing 30-second WebSocket API timeout without waiting in real time.
  - asserts `pendingWsRequests` is `1` before timeout and `0` after timeout.
  - asserts the rejected send and readiness diagnostic contain bounded `OneBot WebSocket API timeout: send_group_msg`.
  - asserts readiness does not expose the seeded group/platform numeric identifier.

Documentation updates in this slice:

- `docs/local-container-acceptance.md`
- `docs/loop-state-recovery.md`
- `docs/long-term-development-direction-review.md`

No runtime code change, commit, staging, live SnowLuma/QQ acceptance, or real secret access was performed in this slice.

### Evidence-backed behavior

- An unanswered WebSocket OneBot send API request is removed from `pendingWsRequests` when the existing 30-second timeout fires.
- Timeout diagnostics remain bounded to the action name and do not include platform target parameters.
- Readiness reflects the timeout without leaking seeded platform identifiers.

## Current R8/R9 Update - HTTP OneBot API Malformed Top-Level Response-Container Fallback - 2026-07-07 13:34 CST

### Scope

Strengthened deterministic evidence and runtime guarding for malformed HTTP OneBot API response JSON containers. Previous slices covered malformed send response `data` containers and structured API-error `message` / `wording` containers; this slice covers the top-level API response value itself when `response.json()` returns a string, array, boolean, or null instead of an object.

This is a bounded runtime hardening plus coverage slice. It does not run real SnowLuma/QQ acceptance and does not claim production readiness.

### Commands run in this checkpoint

```bash
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts -t "malformed top-level OneBot API response containers" --silent  # first run failed before the fix
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts -t "malformed top-level OneBot API response containers" --silent
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts --silent
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts tests/integration/e2e-conversation.test.ts --silent
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test:run && git diff --check
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts tests/integration/e2e-conversation.test.ts --silent
git diff --check
```

### Results

- TDD probe reproduced the bug: targeted `pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts -t "malformed top-level OneBot API response containers" --silent` failed because a `null` API response produced `Cannot read properties of null (reading 'status')` instead of a bounded `Unknown error` diagnostic.
- After the fix, the same targeted command passed with `1 passed | 40 skipped`.
- Related OneBot adapter coverage passed: `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts --silent` passed with `41 passed`.
- Related gateway/e2e coverage passed: `pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts tests/integration/e2e-conversation.test.ts --silent` passed with `2 passed` test files and `124 passed` tests.
- Final full deterministic gate passed: `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test:run && git diff --check` exited `0`; `pnpm test:run` reported `71 passed | 1 skipped` test files and `1037 passed | 22 skipped` tests; `git diff --check` produced no whitespace errors.
- Post-doc related gateway/e2e coverage was refreshed again with `124 passed`, followed by `git diff --check` passing.

### Implementation evidence

Changed paths in this slice:

- `src/gateway/onebot-adapter.ts`
  - changes `assertApiOk()` to accept `unknown`, assert the value is a record, and throw bounded `OneBot API error: Unknown error` for non-object API response containers before reading `status` / `retcode`.
- `tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts`
  - adds `falls back for malformed top-level OneBot API response containers`.
  - covers HTTP `response.json()` values that are a secret/platform-containing string, array, boolean, and null.
  - asserts each send failure contains `Unknown error`, does not leak seeded `sk-...` / `qq-...` / bare numeric fragments, does not contain `[object Object]`, and does not expose raw `Cannot read properties` TypeError text through readiness.

Documentation updates in this slice:

- `docs/local-container-acceptance.md`
- `docs/loop-state-recovery.md`
- `docs/long-term-development-direction-review.md`

No commit, staging, live SnowLuma/QQ acceptance, or real secret access was performed in this slice.

### Evidence-backed behavior

- HTTP OneBot API responses whose top-level JSON value is not an object fail closed with bounded `OneBot API error: Unknown error`.
- Top-level malformed API response containers do not expose seeded secret/platform fragments through thrown errors or readiness.
- A `null` API response no longer produces raw JavaScript property-access TypeError diagnostics.

### Remaining risks / gaps

- This is deterministic Fake HTTP/fetch unit evidence, not real SnowLuma/QQ controlled send-path acceptance.
- Worktree remains highly dirty with substantial tracked and untracked WIP; do not broad-stage, delete scratch files, or commit without explicit user permission.

## Current R8 Update - OneBot Outbound Positive Safe-Integer Send-Target Guard - 2026-07-07 12:17 CST

### Scope

Closed a deterministic outbound send-path boundary gap: OneBot send target identifiers were validated as digit strings, then converted with `Number(...)`, which allowed `qq-0` and unsafe integer strings such as `qq-9007199254740993` to reach the OneBot HTTP/WebSocket send call as invalid or rounded numeric targets. The gateway now requires outbound private/group send targets to normalize to positive safe integers before calling `send_private_msg` / `send_group_msg`.

This is a Fake OneBot unit/related-suite hardening slice. It does not run real SnowLuma/QQ acceptance and does not claim production readiness.

### Commands run in this checkpoint

```bash
git status --short
pnpm list @earendil-works/pi-agent-core @earendil-works/pi-ai --depth 0
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts -t "unsafe outbound OneBot target" --silent
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts --silent
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts tests/integration/e2e-conversation.test.ts --silent
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test:run && git diff --check
```

### Results

- Targeted TDD probe reproduced the gap: `pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts -t "unsafe outbound OneBot target" --silent` failed because an unsafe target reached `fetch` instead of being rejected before send.
- Targeted R8 send-target guard passed after the fix: the same command passed with `1 passed | 30 skipped`.
- Related OneBot adapter coverage passed: `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts --silent` passed with `31 passed`.
- Related gateway/e2e coverage passed: `pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts tests/integration/e2e-conversation.test.ts --silent` passed with `2 passed` test files and `114 passed` tests.
- Final full deterministic gate passed: `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test:run && git diff --check` passed with `71 passed | 1 skipped` test files and `1027 passed | 22 skipped` tests; `git diff --check` produced no whitespace errors.

### Implementation evidence

Changed paths in this slice:

- `src/gateway/onebot-adapter.ts`
  - tightens `toOneBotNumericId()` from digit-string-only validation to positive safe-integer validation before returning a numeric OneBot target ID.
  - rejects `0` and unsafe integer strings before any OneBot send call is attempted.
  - keeps the thrown error bounded as `Invalid OneBot <label>: expected <prefix><positive-safe-integer-id>` without echoing the raw target value.
- `tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts`
  - adds `rejects unsafe outbound OneBot target identifiers before sending`.
  - asserts unsafe private target, zero private target, and unsafe group target reject before `fetch` is called.
  - asserts readiness serialization does not contain the seeded unsafe target ID.
- `docs/contracts.md`
  - documents the outbound send-target positive safe-integer boundary.
- `docs/local-container-acceptance.md`
- `docs/loop-state-recovery.md`
- `docs/long-term-development-direction-review.md`

No commit, staging, live SnowLuma/QQ acceptance, or real secret access was performed in this slice.

### Evidence-backed behavior

- Outbound OneBot private/group send targets must be positive safe-integer QQ identifiers.
- `qq-0` and unsafe integer strings are rejected before HTTP/WebSocket API send calls.
- The gateway no longer risks rounded `Number(...)` targets for unsafe integer strings.
- Rejection errors are bounded and do not echo raw target identifiers.

## Current R8 Update - OneBot Positive Safe-Integer Numeric ID Boundary - 2026-07-07 11:50 CST

### Scope

Closed a OneBot/QQ identifier normalization gap: top-level numeric `message_id`, `user_id`, and `group_id` values were previously too permissive if they were finite numbers, which allowed negative or fractional numeric values to become polluted synthetic platform IDs such as double-hyphen user IDs or fractional group/message IDs. The runtime now treats only positive safe-integer numeric values as valid platform identifiers in these bounded OneBot channels; malformed numeric values fall back to local/unknown identifiers and must not trigger Pi/context/action/outbound side effects.

This slice is deterministic OneBot boundary hardening only. It does not claim real SnowLuma/QQ runtime soak, does not change memory governance semantics, and does not make production-ready completion claims.

### Commands run in this checkpoint


## Current R9 Update - Runtime Structured-Log Prefixed Numeric Platform-ID Redaction - 2026-07-07 11:32 CST

### Scope

Closed a runtime operator-output redaction gap: repository, governance CLI, and worker diagnostic redactors had already been widened for prefixed numeric platform-ID fields such as `targetUserId`, `recipientGroupIds`, and `ownerMessageId`, but the pino structured-log hook still only recognized unprefixed numeric ID fields such as `userId`, `senderIds`, `group_ids`, and `platformIds`. A runtime log payload containing numeric QQ/platform IDs under prefixed field names could therefore reach operator logs raw even though other durable/display boundaries were safer.

This slice only changes structured runtime log sanitization. It does not change stored DB rows, platform adapter semantics, worker scheduling, governance CLI filters, or live SnowLuma/QQ acceptance behavior.

## Current R9 Update - Worker Soak Machine-Readable JSON Stdout - 2026-07-07 09:40 CST

### Scope

Closed a narrow R9 operator-output gap in the opt-in local worker scheduler soak command. Manual `pnpm --silent ops:worker-soak -- --duration-ms=1500 --interval-ms=50` showed the soak succeeded, but scheduler `info` logs were emitted to stdout before/after the JSON when the runtime log level was not forced to `fatal`, making the documented JSON output awkward for machine parsing.

This slice keeps normal app scheduler logging unchanged and only injects a silent logger for the synthetic worker-soak scheduler path. It does not change durable worker semantics, job schema, retry behavior, health criteria, or live SnowLuma/QQ acceptance behavior.

### Commands run in this checkpoint

```bash
pnpm --silent ops:worker-soak -- --duration-ms=1500 --interval-ms=50
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm exec vitest run tests/integration/ops-maintenance-cli.test.ts -t "machine-readable worker-soak" --silent
pnpm exec vitest run tests/integration/ops-maintenance-cli.test.ts tests/unit/workers/scheduler.test.ts tests/unit/workers/background.test.ts tests/unit/storage/job-repository.test.ts --silent
pnpm --silent ops:worker-soak -- --duration-ms=1500 --interval-ms=50
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test:run && git diff --check
```

### Results

- Pre-fix manual worker-soak command exited `0` and returned healthy aggregate evidence, but stdout contained scheduler info logs as well as JSON.

- `docs/long-term-development-direction-review.md`

The storage behavior under test is implemented in:

- `src/storage/job-repository.ts`
  - `fail()` now computes retry scheduling through `const shouldRetry = !options.terminal && row.attempts < row.max_attempts`.

No commit, staging, live SnowLuma/QQ acceptance, or real secret access was performed in this slice.

### Evidence-backed behavior

- Terminal repository failures move the job directly to `failed` with one redacted failed attempt instead of rescheduling it despite remaining `max_attempts`.
- A later claim returns `null`, proving the terminal failed job is not re-queued.
- The job row preserves `status='failed'`, `attempts=1`, `maxAttempts=3`, cleared lease fields, redacted error evidence, and terminal timestamps.
- A single failed `job_attempts` row is persisted and SQLite foreign keys remain clean.
- Default non-terminal retry behavior remains covered by existing job repository and worker tests.

## Current R6 Update - Unsupported Durable Job Terminal Failure - 2026-07-07 09:24 CST

### Scope

Closed a narrow R6 durable-worker observability/control gap: unsupported durable job types were rejected before handler execution, but with `maxAttempts > 1` they still re-entered the pending queue and could retry deterministic bad input until max attempts. This slice makes unsupported durable job types terminal failures after the first claimed attempt, while preserving redacted returned errors, job/attempt rows, heartbeat details, and raw local job type visibility for operator diagnosis.

This is durable worker retry semantics hardening only. It does not change supported worker handlers, scheduler intervals, job schema, or live SnowLuma/QQ acceptance behavior.

### Commands run in this checkpoint

```bash
date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z %z'
git status --short

pnpm typecheck
pnpm lint
pnpm test:run
git diff --check
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/workers/background.test.ts -t "unsupported durable job types" --silent
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/workers/background.test.ts tests/unit/storage/job-repository.test.ts tests/integration/ops-maintenance-cli.test.ts --silent
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test:run && git diff --check
```

- Targeted TDD first showed retry churn for an unsupported durable job with `maxAttempts: 3`: a second `processNext()` returned another failed result instead of `null`.
- After adding terminal failure support for this path, targeted `pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/workers/background.test.ts -t "unsupported durable job types" --silent` passed with `3 passed | 15 skipped`.
- Related worker/repository/ops coverage passed: `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/workers/background.test.ts tests/unit/storage/job-repository.test.ts tests/integration/ops-maintenance-cli.test.ts --silent` passed with `45 passed`.

### Implementation evidence

Changed paths in this slice:

- `src/storage/job-repository.ts`
  - adds a bounded `terminal` option to `JobRepository.fail()` so selected permanent failures can skip retry rescheduling while preserving existing default retry behavior.

## Current R9 Update - Worker Soak Unhealthy Gate Semantics - 2026-07-07 09:10 CST

### Scope

Closed a narrow R9 operator-diagnostic gap in the opt-in local worker scheduler soak command. Before this slice, a too-short or otherwise unhealthy `worker-soak` run printed `success: false` aggregate evidence but still exited with status `0`, making it weak as an operator/CI-style gate. The command now preserves the same redacted aggregate JSON output but sets a non-zero exit code when the aggregate health criteria fail.

This is local synthetic ops hardening only. It does not change durable worker handlers, job schema, production scheduling intervals, or live SnowLuma/QQ acceptance behavior.

### Commands run in this checkpoint

```bash
date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z %z'
git status --short
pnpm list @earendil-works/pi-agent-core @earendil-works/pi-ai --depth 0
pnpm typecheck
pnpm lint
git diff --check
pnpm --silent ops:worker-soak -- --duration-ms=1 --interval-ms=1000
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm exec vitest run tests/integration/ops-maintenance-cli.test.ts -t "worker-soak" --silent
pnpm exec vitest run tests/integration/ops-maintenance-cli.test.ts tests/unit/operations/sqlite-maintenance.test.ts --silent
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test:run && git diff --check
```

### Results

- R0 drift check at `2026-07-07 09:00:40 CST +0800`: worktree remains highly dirty; Pi packages resolve as `@earendil-works/pi-agent-core 0.80.2` and `@earendil-works/pi-ai 0.80.2`.
- Baseline/final gates around this slice are green: `pnpm typecheck` passed; `pnpm lint` passed with `0 warnings / 0 errors`; final `pnpm test:run` passed with `71 passed | 1 skipped` test files and `1013 passed | 22 skipped` tests; `git diff --check` passed.
- Pre-fix probe of `pnpm --silent ops:worker-soak -- --duration-ms=1 --interval-ms=1000` showed aggregate JSON with `success: false`, `ticks: 0`, `processed: 0`, pending jobs, no attempts, no heartbeat, clean FK evidence, but process status still `0`.
- Targeted worker-soak coverage passed: `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm exec vitest run tests/integration/ops-maintenance-cli.test.ts -t "worker-soak" --silent` passed with `2 passed | 9 skipped`.
- Related ops coverage passed: `pnpm exec vitest run tests/integration/ops-maintenance-cli.test.ts tests/unit/operations/sqlite-maintenance.test.ts --silent` passed with `14 passed`.

### Implementation evidence

Changed paths in this slice:

- `src/scripts/ops-maintenance.ts`
  - after printing worker-soak aggregate JSON, sets `process.exitCode = 1` when `runWorkerSchedulerSoak()` returns `success: false`;
  - keeps DB close in the existing `finally` path.
- `tests/integration/ops-maintenance-cli.test.ts`
  - adds spawned ops CLI coverage for a deterministic unhealthy worker-soak run with `--duration-ms=1` and `--interval-ms=1000`;
  - asserts non-zero status, empty stderr, redacted stdout, no raw secret/platform leakage, persisted synthetic job rows, zero attempts/heartbeats, and clean `PRAGMA foreign_key_check`.
- `docs/operations.md`
  - documents that unhealthy worker-soak runs print redacted aggregate JSON and exit non-zero.
- `docs/loop-state-recovery.md`
- `docs/long-term-development-direction-review.md`

No commit, staging, live SnowLuma/QQ acceptance, or real secret access was performed in this slice.

### Evidence-backed behavior

- Healthy opt-in worker-soak runs still exit `0` and preserve aggregate-only evidence for jobs, attempts, lease extensions, heartbeat, and FK status.
- Unhealthy worker-soak runs now exit non-zero without losing diagnostic JSON.
- Unhealthy worker-soak stdout redacts secret-like and QQ/platform-ID-like values embedded in explicit DB paths and worker IDs.

## Current R8 Update - WebSocket Event/Echo Collision Boundary - 2026-07-07 07:30 CST

### Scope

Closed a narrow R8 WebSocket ingress classification gap: a real OneBot event packet may legally contain an `echo` field, and the previous pending-response-first ordering could consume such an event as an API response when its `echo` matched an outstanding WebSocket send request. The current adapter classifies any object packet with a string `post_type` as an inbound OneBot event before attempting pending API-response resolution. This keeps supported `post_type: "message"` packets on the event path and leaves pending API requests waiting for an actual non-event response object.

### Commands run in this checkpoint

```bash
date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z %z'
git status --short
pnpm list @earendil-works/pi-agent-core @earendil-works/pi-ai --depth 0
pnpm typecheck
pnpm lint
pnpm test:run
git diff --check
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts -t "pending echo|non-event WebSocket object|unsupported message_type|unsupported message sub_type" --silent
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts tests/integration/e2e-conversation.test.ts -t "WebSocket|pending echo|message packets|OneBot|unsupported message|message sub_type" --silent
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test:run && git diff --check
```

### Results

- R0 drift check at `2026-07-07 07:28:16 CST +0800`: worktree remains highly dirty; Pi packages resolve as `@earendil-works/pi-agent-core 0.80.2` and `@earendil-works/pi-ai 0.80.2`.
- R0/full baseline was green: `pnpm typecheck` passed; `pnpm lint` passed with 29 existing `@typescript-eslint/no-non-null-assertion` warnings and 0 errors; `pnpm test:run` passed with `71 passed | 1 skipped` test files and `1006 passed | 22 skipped` tests; `git diff --check` passed.
- Targeted WebSocket echo-boundary evidence passed: `pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts -t "pending echo|non-event WebSocket object|unsupported message_type|unsupported message sub_type" --silent` passed with `4 passed | 25 skipped`.
- Related gateway/e2e evidence passed: `pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts tests/integration/e2e-conversation.test.ts -t "WebSocket|pending echo|message packets|OneBot|unsupported message|message sub_type" --silent` passed with `40 passed | 70 skipped`.
- Post-doc full deterministic gate passed: `pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test:run && git diff --check` passed with lint at 29 warnings / 0 errors and full suite at `1006 passed | 22 skipped`.

### Implementation evidence

Changed paths in this slice:

- `src/gateway/onebot-adapter.ts`
  - classifies WebSocket object packets with string `post_type` as OneBot events before pending `echo` response matching.
- `tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts`
  - adds a regression proving a supported `post_type: "message"` packet carrying the same `echo` as an outstanding send emits a chat event, preserves the pending request, does not set readiness `lastError`, and resolves the send only when the later non-event API response object arrives.
- `docs/contracts.md`
  - documents event-before-echo classification and the requirement that event packets must not resolve/remove pending API requests.
- `docs/local-container-acceptance.md`
  - records the deterministic Fake OneBot WebSocket coverage for the echo-collision boundary.
- `docs/loop-state-recovery.md`
- `docs/long-term-development-direction-review.md`

No commit, staging, live SnowLuma/QQ acceptance, or real secret access was performed in this slice.

### Evidence-backed behavior

- A supported WebSocket `post_type: "message"` event packet is emitted as `chat.message.received` even if it carries an `echo` equal to a pending API request.
- The pending WebSocket API request remains visible in readiness until a non-event response object with the matching `echo` arrives.
- Unsupported/non-event WebSocket object boundary behavior remains covered alongside the new echo-collision regression.

### Remaining risks / gaps

- This is deterministic Fake WebSocket adapter evidence only; it does not prove real SnowLuma/QQ WebSocket behavior or a live soak.
- The contract only enables supported message events; unsupported future OneBot event types still require explicit durable contracts before handling.
- Worktree remains highly dirty with substantial tracked and untracked WIP; do not broad-stage or commit without explicit user approval.
- Production-ready long-term completion remains unproven.

## Current R6 Update - Durable Worker Unsupported-Type List Observability - 2026-07-07 07:15 CST

### Scope

Closed a narrow R6 worker observability gap found after a fresh R0 baseline: unsupported durable `jobs.type` values already failed visibly when claimed for execution, but in-process `BackgroundWorker.list()` still converted an unsupported raw type to `summary` through `jobToTask()`. That made pending unsupported durable work look like an ordinary supported summary task during local inspection.

The fix preserves the raw durable job `type` in `BackgroundWorker.list()` output while still restricting `enqueue()` to known task types and while still rejecting unsupported claimed durable work before any handler runs. Supported `summary`, `extraction`, `consolidation`, `decay`, `conflict`, `admin_digest`, and `retention` behavior remains unchanged.

```bash
git status --short

pnpm typecheck
pnpm lint
pnpm test:run
git diff --check
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/workers/background.test.ts -t "unsupported durable job types" --silent
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/workers/background.test.ts tests/unit/workers/scheduler.test.ts tests/unit/storage/job-repository.test.ts -t "unsupported durable job types|durable background worker|job|heartbeat|scheduler" --silent
pnpm typecheck
pnpm lint
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test:run && git diff --check
```

- `pnpm typecheck` passed.

## Current R8 Update - OneBot Message Subtype Boundary - 2026-07-07 07:01 CST

### Scope

Closed a narrow R8 OneBot protocol-boundary gap: `post_type: "message"` packets with supported `message_type` but unsupported or malformed `sub_type` values were still converted into internal chat events. The adapter now treats `sub_type` as a bounded protocol classifier: omitted or `null` is accepted as unspecified; private subtypes are limited to `friend`, `group`, `group_self`, and `other`; group subtypes are limited to `normal`, `anonymous`, and `notice`; unsupported, empty, or malformed subtype values are ignored before chat-event emission. This applies to both reverse HTTP and WebSocket paths and keeps unrelated pending WebSocket API requests intact.

### Commands run in this checkpoint

```bash
date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z %z'
git status --short
pnpm list @earendil-works/pi-agent-core @earendil-works/pi-ai --depth 0
pnpm typecheck
pnpm lint
pnpm test:run
git diff --check
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts tests/integration/e2e-conversation.test.ts -t "message subtypes|message sub_type" --silent
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts tests/integration/e2e-conversation.test.ts -t "message subtypes|message sub_type|message packets with unsupported message sub_type" --silent
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts tests/integration/e2e-conversation.test.ts -t "message subtypes|message sub_type|message_type|unsupported message types" --silent
pnpm typecheck
pnpm lint
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test:run && git diff --check
```

### Results

- R0 drift check at `2026-07-07 06:54:09 CST +0800`: worktree remains highly dirty; Pi packages resolve as `@earendil-works/pi-agent-core 0.80.2` and `@earendil-works/pi-ai 0.80.2`.
- R0/full baseline was green: `pnpm typecheck` passed; `pnpm lint` passed with 29 existing `@typescript-eslint/no-non-null-assertion` warnings and 0 errors; `pnpm test:run` passed with `71 passed | 1 skipped` test files and `1001 passed | 22 skipped` tests; `git diff --check` passed.
- TDD subtype probe reproduced the gap: `pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts tests/integration/e2e-conversation.test.ts -t "message subtypes|message sub_type" --silent` failed because unsupported/malformed `sub_type` packets emitted chat events and the E2E path called Pi twice.
- Targeted subtype evidence passed after the fix: `pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts tests/integration/e2e-conversation.test.ts -t "message subtypes|message sub_type|message packets with unsupported message sub_type" --silent` passed with `4 passed | 105 skipped`.
- Related OneBot message-boundary evidence passed: `pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts tests/integration/e2e-conversation.test.ts -t "message subtypes|message sub_type|message_type|unsupported message types" --silent` passed with `5 passed | 104 skipped`.
- `pnpm typecheck` passed.
- `pnpm lint` passed with the known 29 warnings and 0 errors.
- Final post-doc full deterministic gate passed: `pnpm typecheck` passed; `pnpm lint` passed with 29 existing warnings and 0 errors; `pnpm test:run` passed with `71 passed | 1 skipped` test files and `1004 passed | 22 skipped` tests; `git diff --check` passed.

### Implementation evidence

Changed paths in this slice:

- `src/gateway/onebot-adapter.ts`
  - adds explicit `sub_type` handling and rejects unsupported/empty/malformed subtype classifiers before internal chat-event conversion.
- `tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts`
  - adds reverse HTTP and WebSocket adapter evidence that subtype-rejected packets do not emit chat events, adapter errors, readiness leaks, or pending-response side effects.
- `tests/integration/e2e-conversation.test.ts`
  - adds HTTP/E2E evidence that subtype-rejected payloads do not write raw/chat/turn/context/action/execution rows, call Pi, send messages, record event-processing failures, or leak seeded secret/platform fragments into raw events.
- `docs/contracts.md` and `docs/local-container-acceptance.md`
  - document the supported subtype classifier set and no-side-effect boundary.
- `docs/loop-state-recovery.md` and `docs/long-term-development-direction-review.md`
  - record the current evidence.

### Evidence-backed behavior

- Unsupported string subtypes such as `guild` are ignored before chat-event emission.
- Malformed object/numeric subtype values are ignored before chat-event emission and do not leak into readiness or raw-event rows.
- WebSocket subtype-rejected packets do not resolve or remove unrelated pending API requests.
- Valid message payloads without `sub_type` remain accepted as before.

### Remaining risks / gaps

- This does not prove real SnowLuma/QQ live subtype behavior; it is deterministic Fake OneBot HTTP/WS coverage only.
- The supported subtype allowlist is based on current OneBot 11 private/group classifier expectations; future OneBot extension subtypes require explicit contracts before enabling durable handling.
- Worktree remains highly dirty with substantial tracked and untracked WIP; do not broad-stage or commit without explicit user approval.
- Production-ready long-term completion remains unproven.

## Current R4 Update - Prompt Layer Version Token Evidence - 2026-07-07 06:49 CST

### Scope

Closed a narrow R4 context-explainability gap: `ContextBuilder` token budgeting had per-category counts, but did not expose the prompt renderer / estimate versions behind those counts. `ContextBuilder` now attaches `tokenBudget.promptLayers` with versioned per-layer evidence for recent messages, memory context, identity fields, participant context, and the bounded system-prompt estimate. `/why` displays the layer summary when rebuilt context evidence has it, and `ContextTraceRepository` persists prompt-layer evidence inside the stored token budget with final-guard redaction for layer names and versions.

### Commands run in this checkpoint

```bash
date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z %z'
git status --short

pnpm typecheck
pnpm lint
pnpm test:run

pnpm exec vitest run tests/integration/cli-main.test.ts -t "rebuilt context trace" --silent
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/storage/context-trace-repository.test.ts --silent
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/context/builder.test.ts tests/unit/storage/context-trace-repository.test.ts tests/integration/cli-main.test.ts -t "prompt layer versions|ContextTraceRepository|rebuilt context trace|why command" --silent
pnpm typecheck
pnpm lint
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test:run && git diff --check
```

### Results

- R0 drift check at `2026-07-07 06:40:59 CST +0800`: worktree remains highly dirty; Pi packages resolve as `@earendil-works/pi-agent-core 0.80.2` and `@earendil-works/pi-ai 0.80.2`.
- R0/full baseline was green: `pnpm typecheck` passed; `pnpm lint` passed with 29 existing `@typescript-eslint/no-non-null-assertion` warnings and 0 errors; `pnpm test:run` passed with `71 passed | 1 skipped` test files and `1000 passed | 22 skipped` tests; `git diff --check` passed.

- Targeted builder evidence passed after the fix: `pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/context/builder.test.ts -t "prompt layer versions" --silent` passed with `1 passed | 10 skipped`.
- Targeted `/why` parser evidence passed after the fix: `pnpm exec vitest run tests/integration/cli-main.test.ts -t "rebuilt context trace" --silent` passed with `1 passed | 83 skipped`.
- Targeted durable trace evidence passed after the fix: `pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/storage/context-trace-repository.test.ts --silent` passed with `1 passed`.
- Related context/why evidence passed: `pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/context/builder.test.ts tests/unit/storage/context-trace-repository.test.ts tests/integration/cli-main.test.ts -t "prompt layer versions|ContextTraceRepository|rebuilt context trace|why command" --silent` passed with `8 passed | 88 skipped`.
- `pnpm typecheck` passed.
- `pnpm lint` passed with the known 29 warnings and 0 errors.
- Final post-doc full deterministic gate passed: `pnpm typecheck` passed; `pnpm lint` passed with 29 existing warnings and 0 errors; `pnpm test:run` passed with `71 passed | 1 skipped` test files and `1001 passed | 22 skipped` tests; `git diff --check` passed.

### Implementation evidence

Changed paths in this slice:

- `src/types/context.ts`
  - adds optional `tokenBudget.promptLayers` evidence with layer name, version, and token count.
- `src/context/builder.ts`
  - emits versioned token evidence for `recent_messages`, `memory_context`, `identity_fields`, `participant_context`, and `system_prompt_estimate`.
- `src/cli/main.ts`
  - prints prompt-layer summaries in `/why` output when the context trace includes them.

  - add TDD evidence for prompt-layer token accounting, `/why` display, and durable redaction.
- `docs/context-orchestration.md`, `docs/pi-integration.md`, `docs/loop-state-recovery.md`, and `docs/long-term-development-direction-review.md`
  - document and record the current evidence.

### Evidence-backed behavior

- `ContextBuilder` token budgets now include versioned prompt-layer evidence.
- Prompt-layer token counts sum to `tokenBudget.used`; identity-field and participant-context layers sum to the identity breakdown.
- Rebuilt `/why` output displays prompt-layer versions without printing raw recent-message text or memory content.
- Stored context traces redact prompt-layer name/version narrative before persistence while preserving local lookup IDs and numeric token counts.

### Remaining risks / gaps

- The system-prompt layer remains a bounded estimate, although it is now explicitly versioned as `bounded-system-estimate-v1`.
- The version strings describe current renderer/estimate contracts; there is not yet a centralized prompt-renderer registry shared by `PiAdapter` and `ContextBuilder`.

## Current R4/Pi Update - Participant Group Card and Role Prompt Boundary - 2026-07-07 06:38 CST

### Scope

Closed a narrow R4/Pi prompt-boundary gap: participant display names were structured and redacted in Pi prompts, but participant group cards and platform roles were not rendered, and `ContextBuilder` participant budgeting did not count group-card text. `PiAdapter` now renders explicit group participant `role` and `group_card` metadata as bounded structured fields, redacting/neutralizing group cards with the same data-literal boundary as display names. `ContextBuilder` identity token budgeting now follows that participant line shape. Raw platform account IDs remain excluded from ordinary participant prompt context.

## Current R4 Update - Group Participant Context Budgeting - 2026-07-07 06:28 CST

### Scope

Closed a narrow R4 context-orchestration gap recorded by the previous checkpoint: `ContextBuilder` did not populate participant context, even though `ContextPack` and `PiAdapter` already support structured group participants. `BuildContextInput` now accepts already-resolved `participants`, carries them into the `ContextPack`, records `participant_context` as injected identity evidence for group turns, and includes the current Pi prompt adapter's participant line shape (`display_name=...` plus owner/admin/trusted flags) in identity token budgeting. ## Current R4 Update - Context Token-Budget Prompt Rendering - 2026-07-07 06:20 CST

## Current R6/R7 Update - Job Structured Numeric Platform-ID Redaction - 2026-07-07 06:07 CST

### Scope

Closed a durable/display job diagnostic gap where numeric platform-shaped identifiers in structured job result, job-attempt result, and worker-heartbeat detail fields such as `senderId`, `group_ids`, and `messageId` could remain raw. `JobRepository.complete()` and `JobRepository.heartbeat()` now apply path-aware numeric platform-ID redaction inside structured diagnostics before persistence, while leaving ordinary counters intact. Spawned governance inspection coverage now proves these fields remain redacted in included job/attempt/heartbeat output. Raw job IDs, worker IDs, idempotency keys, and payloads remain local control/lookup evidence and are redacted at display/share boundaries.

## Current R9 Update - Runtime Structured-Log Dynamic Object-Key Redaction - 2026-07-07 04:49 CST

## Current R9 Update - Runtime Structured-Log Plural ID Array Redaction - 2026-07-07 04:38 CST

### Scope

Closed a runtime structured-log display gap where numeric platform-shaped values
inside plural ID arrays were not treated as platform identifiers.
`sanitizeLogValueForOutput()` already redacted secret-like text, prefixed platform
strings, singular numeric ID fields, and stack fields. This slice extends the
numeric platform-ID field detector to plural snake_case and camelCase keys such
as `senderIds`, `group_ids`, and `platformIds`, so pino hook output does not
print numeric QQ/platform-shaped identifiers from those arrays.

### Commands run in this checkpoint

```bash
git status --short
pnpm list @earendil-works/pi-agent-core @earendil-works/pi-ai --depth 0
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test:run && git diff --check
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/logger/index.test.ts -t "plural ID arrays" --silent
pnpm lint && pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/logger/index.test.ts --silent
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test:run && git diff --check
```

- Targeted post-fix evidence passed: `tests/unit/logger/index.test.ts -t "plural ID arrays" --silent` reported `1 passed | 10 skipped`.
- Related R9 logger suite passed: `pnpm lint && pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/logger/index.test.ts --silent` passed with lint at 29 existing warnings / 0 errors and logger tests at `11 passed`.

## Current R1/R9 Update - Local Smoke Script Recovery and Diagnostic Redaction - 2026-07-07 04:29 CST

### Scope

Recovered the deterministic local smoke operator path and removed a direct-console
leak on smoke failures. `scripts/smoke-test.ts` had drifted behind the current
`ToolRegistry` contract by registering `smoke_tool` with an unresolved string
handler and the old sandbox-policy field shape. It now registers a resolved
in-process handler with the current `SandboxPolicy` shape, exports a bounded
smoke diagnostic formatter, guards direct execution so tests can import the
formatter without running the smoke path, and redacts smoke/fatal error output
before direct `console.error`.

### Commands run in this checkpoint

```bash
git status --short
pnpm list @earendil-works/pi-agent-core @earendil-works/pi-ai --depth 0
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test:run && git diff --check
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/scripts/smoke-test.test.ts --silent
pnpm --silent smoke
pnpm lint && pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/scripts/smoke-test.test.ts tests/unit/tools/registry.test.ts --silent
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test:run && git diff --check
```

### Results

- R0 drift check: worktree remains highly dirty; Pi packages resolve as `@earendil-works/pi-agent-core 0.80.2` and `@earendil-works/pi-ai 0.80.2`.
- R0/full baseline before this slice was green: `pnpm typecheck` passed; `pnpm lint` passed with 29 existing `@typescript-eslint/no-non-null-assertion` warnings and 0 errors; `pnpm test:run` passed with `69 passed | 1 skipped` test files and `989 passed | 22 skipped` tests; `git diff --check` passed.
- Targeted TDD reproduced the drift/leak: `tests/unit/scripts/smoke-test.test.ts --silent` failed because spawned `scripts/smoke-test.ts` exited `1` on `ToolRegistry.register()` and stderr included raw stack/source-path diagnostics; the formatter expectation also failed because no bounded formatter was exported yet.
- Targeted post-fix evidence passed: `tests/unit/scripts/smoke-test.test.ts --silent` reported `2 passed`.
- Package-script smoke evidence passed: `pnpm --silent smoke` exited `0`, printed `✨ All smoke tests passed!`, and kept stderr empty.
- Related R1/R9 suite passed: `pnpm lint && pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/scripts/smoke-test.test.ts tests/unit/tools/registry.test.ts --silent` passed with lint at 29 existing warnings / 0 errors and tests at `11 passed`.
- Final full gate passed:
  - `pnpm typecheck`: passed.
  - `pnpm lint`: passed with the same 29 existing warnings and 0 errors.
  - `pnpm test:run`: passed with `69 passed | 1 skipped` test files and `991 passed | 22 skipped` tests.
  - `git diff --check`: passed.
- Post-doc full gate after this checkpoint update passed at `2026-07-07 04:29 CST`: `pnpm typecheck` passed; `pnpm lint` passed with 29 existing warnings and 0 errors; `pnpm test:run` passed with `69 passed | 1 skipped` test files and `991 passed | 22 skipped` tests; `git diff --check` passed.

### Implementation evidence

Changed paths in this slice:

- `scripts/smoke-test.ts`
  - exports `formatSmokeErrorForConsole()` and `smokeTest()`;
  - guards direct execution with `import.meta.url` vs `process.argv[1]`;
  - formats smoke/fatal direct-console diagnostics with deterministic secret/platform redaction and stack suppression;
  - registers `smoke_tool` with a resolved in-process handler and current `SandboxPolicy` fields.
- `tests/unit/scripts/smoke-test.test.ts`
  - adds spawned smoke-script evidence that the deterministic local smoke path exits `0`, prints success output, and leaves stderr empty;
  - adds formatter evidence that seeded secret-like text, QQ/platform-ID-like values, stack frames, source paths, and `node_modules` paths are not displayed.
- `docs/operations.md`
  - documents `pnpm --silent smoke` as a deterministic local sanity check and records its redaction expectations.
- `docs/long-term-development-direction-review.md`
  - updates the active evidence baseline to include this R1/R9 smoke recovery slice and the current `991 passed | 22 skipped` deterministic suite count.
- `docs/loop-state-recovery.md`
  - records this checkpoint and next step.

- Smoke failure diagnostics are bounded display strings; seeded API-key-like text, QQ/platform-ID-like values, stack frames, source paths, and `node_modules` paths are redacted/suppressed before direct console output.

## Current R7 Update - Governance CLI Memory Record Classification Display Redaction - 2026-07-07 04:17 CST

### Scope

Closed spawned governance CLI display leaks for dirty/legacy `memory_records`
classification fields. Current migrated SQLite constrains `scope`, `visibility`,
`sensitivity`, `authority`, `kind`, and `state`, but dirty/legacy DBs or manual
repairs can still contain free-text values if CHECK constraints were bypassed
before inspection. `GovernanceCLI.redactMemoryRecordForDisplay()` now treats
those record fields as display strings and redacts them for `show-memory` and
`export-memory`; `list-memory` also redacts human-readable scope/state/visibility
lines. Exact raw DB values remain unchanged for local operator evidence and valid
filters still use finite enums.

## Current R7 Update - Governance CLI Memory Source/Revision Classification Display Redaction - 2026-07-07 03:50 CST

### Scope

Closed a spawned governance CLI inspection leak for legacy memory evidence
classification values. Current migrated SQLite constrains
`memory_sources.source_type` and `memory_revisions.change_type`, but dirty/legacy
DBs or manual repairs can still contain free-text values if constraints were
bypassed before inspection. `GovernanceCLI.showMemory()` now treats those fields
as display strings and redacts them before returning JSON, while exact raw DB
values remain unchanged for local operator evidence and valid filters still use
current finite enums.

### Scope

Closed a spawned governance CLI inspection leak in `show-memory` source evidence.
`GovernanceCLI.showMemory()` already redacted `memory_sources.memory_id` and
`source_id`, but returned `extracted_by` directly. A legacy/free-text extractor
value containing secret-like or QQ/platform-ID-like fragments could therefore
appear in JSON output even though raw DB rows should remain local-only evidence.

### Commands run in this checkpoint

## Current R9 Update - SQLite Verbose SQL Direct Console Redaction - 2026-07-07 02:40 CST

### Scope

Closed a direct-console operator-diagnostic leak in the SQLite verbose hook.
`better-sqlite3` calls the configured `verbose` function directly, and the old
`verbose: config.verbose ? console.log : undefined` wiring could print SQL text
containing credential-like fragments, user message content, or QQ/platform-ID-like
values outside the structured pino redaction boundary. This slice routes verbose
SQL display through a small redaction wrapper before calling `console.log`.

### Commands run in this checkpoint

```bash
git status --short
pnpm list @earendil-works/pi-agent-core @earendil-works/pi-ai --depth 0
pnpm typecheck
pnpm lint
pnpm test:run
git diff --check
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/storage/database.test.ts -t "verbose SQL" --silent
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/storage/database.test.ts --silent
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/storage/database.test.ts tests/unit/operations/sqlite-maintenance.test.ts tests/integration/ops-maintenance-cli.test.ts --silent
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test:run && git diff --check
```

### Results

- Baseline drift check: worktree remains highly dirty; Pi packages resolve as `@earendil-works/pi-agent-core 0.80.2` and `@earendil-works/pi-ai 0.80.2`.
- Current R0/full gate evidence in this continuation is green:
  - `pnpm typecheck`: passed.
  - `pnpm lint`: passed with the same 29 existing `@typescript-eslint/no-non-null-assertion` warnings and 0 errors.
  - `pnpm test:run`: passed with `68 passed | 1 skipped` test files and `982 passed | 22 skipped` tests.
  - `git diff --check`: passed.
- Targeted evidence passed: `pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/storage/database.test.ts -t "verbose SQL" --silent` reported `1 passed | 19 skipped`.
- Full database unit suite passed: `pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/storage/database.test.ts --silent` reported `20 passed`.
- Related storage/ops suite passed: `pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/storage/database.test.ts tests/unit/operations/sqlite-maintenance.test.ts tests/integration/ops-maintenance-cli.test.ts --silent` reported `33 passed`.
- Final post-documentation full gate also passed: `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm lint` with the same 29 warnings / 0 errors, `pnpm test:run` with `68 passed | 1 skipped` test files and `982 passed | 22 skipped` tests, and `git diff --check` all exited 0.

### Implementation evidence

Changed paths in this slice:

- `src/storage/database.ts`
  - replaces raw `verbose: console.log` with `verbose: logVerboseSql`;
  - redacts secret-like text through `redactSecretsInText()` before display;
  - redacts QQ/platform-ID-like fragments, including `qq-...` and bare 8-12 digit IDs, before direct console output.
- `tests/unit/storage/database.test.ts`
  - adds `should redact verbose SQL console output`, which spies on `console.log`, executes verbose SQL containing seeded API-key-like and QQ/platform-ID-like text, and asserts redaction markers are emitted while raw seeded values are absent.
- `docs/operations.md`
  - documents SQLite verbose SQL as a direct-console boundary and forbids raw `console.log` verbose wiring.
- `docs/long-term-development-direction-review.md`
  - updates the active evidence baseline to include this R9 diagnostic slice and the current `982 passed | 22 skipped` deterministic suite count.
- `docs/loop-state-recovery.md`
  - records this checkpoint and next step.

### Evidence-backed behavior

- `initDatabase({ verbose: true })` no longer sends raw SQL text directly to `console.log`.
- Verbose SQL display redacts seeded API-key-like/secret-assignment text and QQ/platform-ID-like values before operator output.
- Existing storage, ops, and full deterministic gates remain green after the code/test change.

### Remaining risks / gaps

## Current R8/R9 Update - OneBot Event-Handler Direct Console Diagnostic Redaction - 2026-07-07 02:27 CST

### Scope

Closed another direct-console operator-diagnostic leak in the OneBot gateway.
`handleHttpEvent()` catches event-listener failures and previously printed
`console.error('Failed to handle OneBot event:', new Error(redactedMessage))`.
Even with redacted message text, Node console rendering of an `Error` argument
prints stack frames, source paths, and `node_modules` frames. This slice keeps
redacted readiness/error-event message evidence while replacing direct console
failure display with a bounded redacted diagnostic string.

```bash
pnpm lint
pnpm test:run

pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts --silent
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/gateway/onebot-adapter.test.ts tests/integration/e2e-conversation.test.ts --silent
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test:run && git diff --check
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test:run && git diff --check
```

## Current R9 Update - Runtime Structured-Log Stack Suppression - 2026-07-07 01:36 CST

### Scope

Closed a runtime structured-log operator-diagnostic leak for pino logger output. Previous logger redaction already replaced secret-like and QQ/platform-ID-like text in Error messages and stacks, but it still retained stack frames, source paths, and `node_modules` paths. A first targeted probe fixed direct `Error` instances, then the full gate output exposed a stronger real path: many runtime call sites first convert errors into plain `{ message, stack, name }` objects, so the stack string still reached pino-pretty. This slice suppresses both direct `Error.stack` and plain structured `stack` fields with `[REDACTED:stack]` before write.

### Commands run in this checkpoint

```bash
git status --short
pnpm list @earendil-works/pi-agent-core @earendil-works/pi-ai --depth 0
pnpm typecheck
pnpm lint
pnpm test:run
git diff --check
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/logger/index.test.ts -t "stack" --silent
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/logger/index.test.ts --silent
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test:run && git diff --check
```

### Results

- Baseline drift check: worktree remains highly dirty; Pi packages resolve as `@earendil-works/pi-agent-core 0.80.2` and `@earendil-works/pi-ai 0.80.2`.
- Initial R0/full gate evidence at this continuation was green: `pnpm typecheck` passed, `pnpm lint` passed with the same 29 existing `@typescript-eslint/no-non-null-assertion` warnings and 0 errors, `pnpm test:run` passed with `66 passed | 1 skipped` test files and `972 passed | 22 skipped` tests, and `git diff --check` passed.
- Targeted TDD first reproduced the direct Error stack leak: `tests/unit/logger/index.test.ts -t "stack"` failed because sanitized Error stack output still contained stack frames, source paths, and `node_modules` paths.
- After the first fix, the full gate output exposed the plain structured stack-field path in summary-worker/index logs; the test was extended to cover ordinary `{ error: { message, stack, name } }` objects and failed before the second fix.
- Targeted post-fix stack evidence passed: `tests/unit/logger/index.test.ts -t "stack"` reported `4 passed | 6 skipped`.
- Full logger suite passed: `tests/unit/logger/index.test.ts` reported `10 passed`.
- Final full gate after the code/test/operations-doc/direction-review updates passed:
  - `pnpm typecheck`: passed.
  - `pnpm lint`: passed with the same 29 existing `@typescript-eslint/no-non-null-assertion` warnings and 0 errors.
  - `pnpm test:run`: passed with `66 passed | 1 skipped` test files and `975 passed | 22 skipped` tests.
  - `git diff --check`: passed.
- After writing this loop-state checkpoint, a docs-only `git diff --check` also passed.

### Implementation evidence

- `src/logger/index.ts`
  - changes direct `Error` sanitization to keep redacted `message`/`name` while replacing `stack` with `[REDACTED:stack]`;
  - adds stack-key detection so plain structured `stack` string fields are also replaced before pino writes.

  - adds sanitizer and actual pino hook coverage for plain structured `{ error: { stack } }` fields, matching summary-worker/index runtime logging patterns.

## Current R7/R9 Update - Redacted Governance CLI Config-Load Failures - 2026-07-07 01:24 CST

### Scope

Closed a spawned governance CLI operator-diagnostic leak in the configuration load path. Before this slice, `src/cli/main.ts` loaded config at module top level, before the `program.parseAsync()` redacted catch path. A sanitized manual probe showed that an invalid `LOG_LEVEL` caused `src/cli/main.ts --help` to exit 1 while stderr contained the raw seeded env value, source-path fragments, the config error class name, raw validation preamble, and stack frames. This slice makes CLI DB config loading lazy inside command execution and removes config-loader stderr side effects so config validation failures flow through redacted command/error handling.

### Commands run in this checkpoint

```bash
git status --short
pnpm list @earendil-works/pi-agent-core @earendil-works/pi-ai --depth 0
pnpm typecheck
pnpm lint
pnpm test:run
git diff --check
# sanitized manual probe for invalid LOG_LEVEL before and after the fix
pnpm exec vitest run tests/integration/cli-main.test.ts -t "config-load failures" --silent
pnpm exec vitest run tests/integration/cli-main.test.ts --silent
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/config/index.test.ts tests/unit/config/napcat-config.test.ts tests/unit/scripts/deploy-napcat.test.ts --silent
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test:run && git diff --check
```

### Results

- Baseline drift check: worktree remains highly dirty; Pi packages resolve as `@earendil-works/pi-agent-core 0.80.2` and `@earendil-works/pi-ai 0.80.2`.
- Initial R0/full gate evidence at this continuation was green: `pnpm typecheck` passed, `pnpm lint` passed with the same 29 existing `@typescript-eslint/no-non-null-assertion` warnings and 0 errors, `pnpm test:run` passed with `66 passed | 1 skipped` test files and `971 passed | 22 skipped` tests, and `git diff --check` passed.
- Initial sanitized manual probe reproduced the leak without printing the raw stderr contents into this doc: the invalid config path exited non-zero and leak-marker checks found the raw seeded env fragments, `src/config`, `src/cli`, `ConfigValidationError`, `Configuration validation failed`, and stack frames.
- Targeted post-fix evidence passed: `tests/integration/cli-main.test.ts -t "config-load failures"` reported `1 passed | 76 skipped`.
- Manual post-fix probe showed top-level help exits 0 with stderr empty and no leak markers, while `list-memory` with invalid config exits 1 with empty stdout, concise redacted stderr, and no leak markers.
- Full spawned CLI parser/inspection suite passed: `tests/integration/cli-main.test.ts` reported `77 passed`.
- Related config/deploy suite passed: `tests/unit/config/index.test.ts`, `tests/unit/config/napcat-config.test.ts`, and `tests/unit/scripts/deploy-napcat.test.ts` reported `45 passed`.
- Final full gate after the code/test/documentation updates passed:
  - `pnpm typecheck`: passed.
  - `pnpm lint`: passed with the same 29 existing `@typescript-eslint/no-non-null-assertion` warnings and 0 errors.
  - `pnpm test:run`: passed with `66 passed | 1 skipped` test files and `972 passed | 22 skipped` tests.
  - `git diff --check`: passed.

### Implementation evidence

Changed paths in this slice:

- `src/cli/main.ts`
  - removes module-top-level `loadConfig()`;
  - adds lazy DB path loading through `getDbPath()` for DB-backed command actions and `withGovernanceCli()`;
  - keeps `program.parseAsync()` wrapped by the existing redacted `printError()` path.
- `src/config/index.ts`
  - removes raw stderr printing of zod validation issues from `loadConfig()`; callers receive `ConfigValidationError` and decide how to display redacted diagnostics.
- `tests/integration/cli-main.test.ts`
  - adds `spawns config-load failures without leaking env values or stack traces`;
  - asserts invalid config does not leak seeded secret/platform fragments, raw env values, source paths, zod issue markers, error class names, or stack frames;
  - proves `--help` remains read-only and available under invalid DB config, while DB-backed `list-memory` fails redacted and leaves the intended DB unchanged with clean SQLite FKs.
- `docs/operations.md`
  - documents redacted config-load failures and lazy DB-backed CLI config loading.
- `docs/long-term-development-direction-review.md`
  - updates the active evidence baseline to include this R7/R9 operator-diagnostic slice.
- `docs/loop-state-recovery.md`
  - records this checkpoint and next step.

### Evidence-backed behavior

- Invalid governance CLI runtime configuration no longer prints raw zod issue dumps, error class names, source paths, or stack traces.
- Top-level help does not require loading DB config and remains available under invalid runtime configuration.
- DB-backed commands still fail closed when config is invalid, but display only concise redacted stderr.
- The failing spawned command does not mutate the intended test database and SQLite FKs remain clean.

## Current R7/R9 Update - Redacted Governance CLI DB-Open Failures - 2026-07-07 01:10 CST

### Scope

Closed a spawned governance CLI operator-diagnostic leak: when `LETHEBOT_DB_PATH` pointed into a missing directory, async command actions such as `summarize-governance-health` rejected during `initDatabase()` before command-local `try/catch` blocks ran. Commander then allowed Node to print a raw `better-sqlite3` stack trace with `node_modules`, `src/storage/database.ts`, and `src/cli/main.ts` frames. This slice switches the CLI entrypoint to `program.parseAsync()` with a top-level redacted catch path and adds spawned evidence for both a `withGovernanceCli` command and a direct `initDatabase` command.

```bash
pnpm lint
```

## Current R6 Update - Error Heartbeat Clears After Later Successful Work - 2026-07-07 00:46 CST

### Scope

Added deterministic evidence for the recovery side of the retained worker-error heartbeat behavior. The previous R6 slice fixed the edge where a later empty durable poll erased a final failure heartbeat. This slice proves the retained error heartbeat is not sticky forever: after an empty poll preserves the failure linkage, the same worker can claim later durable work, transition through the normal running path, complete successfully, and clear `worker_heartbeats` back to `idle` with no current job or stale error details.

```bash

pnpm lint
pnpm test:run

pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/workers/background.test.ts tests/unit/storage/job-repository.test.ts tests/unit/workers/scheduler.test.ts tests/integration/ops-maintenance-cli.test.ts --silent
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test:run && git diff --check
```

### Implementation evidence

- `tests/unit/workers/background.test.ts`
  - adds `should clear a retained error heartbeat after successfully processing later durable work`;
  - creates a final-failed conflict job, confirms an empty poll preserves the error heartbeat, then enqueues a summary job and confirms successful processing clears heartbeat state to `idle` / `current_job_id=NULL` / `details=NULL`.

- The retained `error` heartbeat keeps failed-job linkage across the empty poll.
- Later ready work can still be claimed by the same worker.
- After later successful durable work, `worker_heartbeats` returns to `idle`, clears `current_job_id`, and clears stale error `details`.
- The later successful job has one completed attempt, persisted result evidence, and SQLite FKs remain clean.

## Current R6 Update - Preserve Final Worker Failure Heartbeat Across Empty Polls - 2026-07-07 00:41 CST

### Scope

Strengthened durable background worker failure observability. Before this slice, a max-attempt durable worker failure wrote a redacted `error` heartbeat with current-job linkage, but a later empty `processNext()` poll immediately overwrote that row with `idle` / `current_job_id = NULL`. That made the latest worker heartbeat lose the failure linkage even though the failed `jobs` and `job_attempts` rows remained durable. The fix keeps an existing `error` heartbeat intact across empty polls; the heartbeat transitions again when the worker claims new work and writes `running`, then returns to `idle` after successful completion.

### Commands run in this checkpoint

```bash
git status --short
pnpm list @earendil-works/pi-agent-core @earendil-works/pi-ai --depth 0
pnpm typecheck
pnpm lint
pnpm test:run
git diff --check
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/workers/background.test.ts -t "empty durable poll" --silent
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/workers/background.test.ts tests/unit/storage/job-repository.test.ts tests/unit/workers/scheduler.test.ts tests/integration/ops-maintenance-cli.test.ts --silent
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/workers/background.test.ts tests/unit/storage/job-repository.test.ts tests/unit/workers/scheduler.test.ts tests/integration/ops-maintenance-cli.test.ts --silent && git diff --check
pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm test:run && git diff --check
```

### Results

- Baseline drift check: worktree remains highly dirty; Pi packages resolve as `@earendil-works/pi-agent-core 0.80.2` and `@earendil-works/pi-ai 0.80.2`.
- Initial R0/full gate evidence at this continuation was green: `pnpm typecheck` passed, `pnpm lint` passed with the same 29 existing `@typescript-eslint/no-non-null-assertion` warnings and 0 errors, `pnpm test:run` passed with `66 passed | 1 skipped` test files and `968 passed | 22 skipped` tests, and `git diff --check` passed.
- Initial targeted TDD run reproduced the bug: `tests/unit/workers/background.test.ts -t "empty durable poll"` failed with `1 failed | 14 skipped` because a later empty durable poll changed the worker heartbeat from `error` with `current_job_id=<failed job>` to `idle` with `current_job_id=NULL`.
- Targeted post-fix evidence passed: `tests/unit/workers/background.test.ts -t "empty durable poll"` reported `1 passed | 14 skipped`.
- Related R6/R9 worker/job/ops suite passed: `tests/unit/workers/background.test.ts`, `tests/unit/storage/job-repository.test.ts`, `tests/unit/workers/scheduler.test.ts`, and `tests/integration/ops-maintenance-cli.test.ts` reported `4 passed` test files and `49 passed` tests.
- Targeted gate passed: `pnpm typecheck`, `pnpm lint`, related R6/R9 suite, and `git diff --check` all exited 0; lint stayed at 29 warnings / 0 errors.
- Final full gate after the code/test/documentation updates passed:
  - `pnpm typecheck`: passed.
  - `pnpm lint`: passed with the same 29 existing `@typescript-eslint/no-non-null-assertion` warnings and 0 errors.
  - `pnpm test:run`: passed with `66 passed | 1 skipped` test files and `969 passed | 22 skipped` tests.
  - `git diff --check`: passed.

### Implementation evidence

Changed paths in this slice:

- `src/storage/job-repository.ts`
  - adds `getWorkerHeartbeatStatus(workerId)` so durable worker polling can distinguish an existing `error` heartbeat from ordinary idle/running state without reading or displaying sensitive heartbeat details.
- `src/workers/background.ts`
  - `processNextDurable()` no longer overwrites an existing `error` heartbeat with `idle` before an empty claim poll; claiming later work still writes `running`, and completion still writes `idle`.
- `tests/unit/workers/background.test.ts`
  - adds `should preserve final failure heartbeat evidence after a later empty durable poll`;
  - asserts the second empty poll returns `null`, the failed job remains failed, the heartbeat keeps `status='error'`, `current_job_id=<failed job>`, redacted details, and clean SQLite FKs.
- `docs/operations.md`
  - documents the retained `error` heartbeat behavior for durable worker failure inspection.
- `docs/long-term-development-direction-review.md`
  - updates the active evidence baseline to include this R6 failure-visibility slice.
- `docs/loop-state-recovery.md`
  - records this checkpoint and next step.

### Evidence-backed behavior

- A max-attempt durable worker failure remains visible in `jobs`, `job_attempts`, and `worker_heartbeats` after a later empty worker poll.
- `worker_heartbeats.status` stays `error` and `current_job_id` keeps the failed job linkage until real work is claimed again.
- Heartbeat `details.error`, job error, attempt error, and returned `TaskResult.error` use deterministic secret/platform redaction.
- Empty polling does not create extra job attempts or mutate the failed job out of `failed` state.
- SQLite foreign keys remain clean.

### Remaining risks / gaps

- This is deterministic unit/integration evidence, not a real multi-process worker deployment.
- It does not claim all worker observability is complete; it specifically closes the final-failure heartbeat erasure edge while existing tests cover retries, leases, lease extension, unsupported job types, worker-soak aggregates, and CLI inspection redaction.
- Worktree remains highly dirty; do not broad-stage or commit without explicit user approval.
- Real SnowLuma/QQ controlled soak remains manual/opt-in and was not run.

## Current R7/R4 Update - Private `why --conversation` Rejects Group Scope - 2026-07-07 00:28 CST

### Scope

Strengthened the spawned governance CLI `/why` equivalent so conversation-scoped private context rebuilds reject an unexpected `--group`. Before this slice, `pnpm cli why --conversation <private-conversation-id> --type private --group <group-id> --user <user>` could return success by rebuilding a private context that carried group identity fields and group-scoped retrieval hints. That made an operator request look like a valid private explanation while mixing group scope into private context assembly. The fix rejects the invalid private/group scope combination before stored-trace lookup or ContextBuilder rebuild dispatch.

### Commands run in this checkpoint

```bash
git status --short
pnpm list @earendil-works/pi-agent-core @earendil-works/pi-ai --depth 0
pnpm typecheck
pnpm lint
pnpm test:run
git diff --check
pnpm exec vitest run tests/integration/cli-main.test.ts -t "private conversation validation" --silent
pnpm exec vitest run tests/integration/cli-main.test.ts -t "spawns why" --silent
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/cli/governance.test.ts -t "explainContext" --silent
pnpm exec vitest run tests/integration/cli-main.test.ts --silent
pnpm typecheck
pnpm lint
pnpm test:run
git diff --check
```

### Results

- Baseline drift check: worktree remains highly dirty; Pi packages resolve as `@earendil-works/pi-agent-core 0.80.2` and `@earendil-works/pi-ai 0.80.2`.
- Initial R0/full gate evidence at this continuation was green: `pnpm typecheck` passed, `pnpm lint` passed with the same 29 existing `@typescript-eslint/no-non-null-assertion` warnings and 0 errors, `pnpm test:run` passed with `66 passed | 1 skipped` test files and `967 passed | 22 skipped` tests, and `git diff --check` passed.
- Initial targeted TDD run reproduced the bug: `tests/integration/cli-main.test.ts -t "private conversation validation"` failed because the private conversation rebuild with `--group` exited `0` instead of rejecting the invalid group scope.
- Targeted post-fix spawned CLI evidence passed: `tests/integration/cli-main.test.ts -t "private conversation validation"` reported `1 passed | 74 skipped`.
- Related spawned `why` suite passed: `tests/integration/cli-main.test.ts -t "spawns why"` reported `11 passed | 64 skipped`.
- Service-level explainContext suite passed: `tests/unit/cli/governance.test.ts -t "explainContext"` reported `2 passed | 28 skipped`.
- Full spawned CLI suite passed: `tests/integration/cli-main.test.ts` reported `75 passed`.
- Final gate after the test/code/documentation updates passed:
  - `pnpm typecheck`: passed.
  - `pnpm lint`: passed with the same 29 existing `@typescript-eslint/no-non-null-assertion` warnings and 0 errors.
  - `pnpm test:run`: passed with `66 passed | 1 skipped` test files and `968 passed | 22 skipped` tests.
  - `git diff --check`: passed.

### Implementation evidence

Changed paths in this slice:

- `src/cli/governance.ts`
  - `resolveExplainContextOptions()` now rejects `options.conversationId` with `options.conversationType === 'private'` and `options.groupId` using `Group ID is not allowed when --conversation uses --type private` before stored-trace lookup or context rebuild resolution.
- `tests/integration/cli-main.test.ts`
  - adds `spawns why private conversation validation errors with unexpected group scope and no mutation`;
  - calls `why --conversation <sensitive-private-conversation-id> --type private --group <sensitive-group-id> --user user-cli`;
  - asserts non-zero exit, empty stdout, concise stderr, no leaked secret/platform/conversation/group/synthetic rebuild IDs, no stack paths, no DB mutation, and clean SQLite FKs.
- `docs/operations.md`
  - documents that private `why --conversation` rebuilds reject `--group` so private contexts cannot carry group identity fields or group-scoped retrieval hints.
- `docs/long-term-development-direction-review.md`
  - updates the active evidence baseline to include this R7/R4 spawned CLI validation slice.
- `docs/loop-state-recovery.md`
  - records this checkpoint and next step.

### Evidence-backed behavior

- `why --conversation <conversation-id> --type private --group <group-id>` is treated as an invalid private/group scope combination.
- The command fails before stored context trace lookup.
- The command fails before ContextBuilder rebuild dispatch.
- Failure stdout is empty and stderr is concise/redacted.
- Sensitive conversation IDs, sensitive group IDs, seeded API-key-like values, seeded QQ/platform-like values, and synthetic rebuild IDs are not displayed.
- The failure path does not mutate canonical users, raw events, chat messages, agent turns, context traces, audit rows, or memory rows, and SQLite FKs remain clean.

### Remaining risks / gaps

- This is deterministic spawned CLI evidence, not a web UI or live operator session.
- It does not claim all `/why` option combinations are exhaustive; it covers the invalid private rebuild with group scope while existing tests cover stored traces, group stored traces, latest-turn fallback, rebuilt private traces, invalid type, missing turn/no turn, missing conversation type, group conversation missing group scope, mixed turn/conversation ambiguity, redaction, token budget display, and raw DB preservation.
- Worktree remains highly dirty; do not broad-stage or commit without explicit user approval.

### Next exact step

Continue with one narrow evidence-backed slice after a light drift check. ## Current R7 Update - `why` Mixed Turn/Conversation Validation - 2026-07-07 00:10 CST

### Scope

Strengthened the spawned governance CLI `/why` equivalent so turn-scoped and conversation-scoped explanation requests are mutually exclusive. Before this slice, `pnpm cli why --turn <turn-id> --conversation <conversation-id> --type private` could return success by displaying the stored trace for the turn while silently ignoring the conversation selector. That made an ambiguous operator request capable of showing unrelated trace IDs. The fix rejects the mixed request before stored-trace lookup or ContextBuilder rebuild dispatch.

### Implementation evidence

- `src/cli/governance.ts`
  - `resolveExplainContextOptions()` now rejects simultaneous `options.turnId` and `options.conversationId` using `Choose either --turn or --conversation, not both` before stored-trace lookup or context rebuild resolution.

- The failure path does not mutate canonical users, raw events, chat messages, agent turns, context traces, audit rows, or memory rows, and SQLite FKs remain clean.

## Current R7 Update - `why --conversation` Explicit-Type Validation - 2026-07-07 00:02 CST

### Scope

Strengthened the spawned governance CLI `/why` equivalent so a conversation-scoped context rebuild request must provide an explicit conversation type. Before this slice, `pnpm cli why --conversation <id>` could silently fall back to resolving the latest stored turn and return an unrelated context explanation. This violated explainability expectations because a partial conversation-specific request could display unrelated latest-turn trace IDs. The fix rejects incomplete conversation rebuild parameters before latest-turn resolution or context dispatch.

### Commands run in this checkpoint

```bash
git status --short
pnpm list @earendil-works/pi-agent-core @earendil-works/pi-ai --depth 0
pnpm typecheck
pnpm lint
pnpm test:run
git diff --check
pnpm exec vitest run tests/integration/cli-main.test.ts -t "conversation-only" --silent
pnpm exec vitest run tests/integration/cli-main.test.ts -t "spawns why" --silent
pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/cli/governance.test.ts -t "explainContext" --silent
pnpm exec vitest run tests/integration/cli-main.test.ts --silent
pnpm typecheck
pnpm lint
pnpm test:run
git diff --check
```

### Results

- Baseline drift check: worktree remains highly dirty; Pi packages resolve as `@earendil-works/pi-agent-core 0.80.2` and `@earendil-works/pi-ai 0.80.2`.
- Initial targeted TDD run reproduced the bug: `tests/integration/cli-main.test.ts -t "conversation-only"` failed because `why --conversation <id>` exited `0` instead of rejecting the incomplete request.
- Targeted post-fix spawned CLI evidence passed: `tests/integration/cli-main.test.ts -t "conversation-only"` reported `1 passed | 71 skipped`.
- Related spawned `why` suite passed: `tests/integration/cli-main.test.ts -t "spawns why"` reported `8 passed | 64 skipped`.
- Service-level explainContext suite passed: `tests/unit/cli/governance.test.ts -t "explainContext"` reported `2 passed | 28 skipped`.
- Full spawned CLI suite passed: `tests/integration/cli-main.test.ts` reported `72 passed`.
- Final gate after the test/code/documentation updates passed:
  - `pnpm typecheck`: passed.
  - `pnpm lint`: passed with the same 29 existing `@typescript-eslint/no-non-null-assertion` warnings and 0 errors.
  - `pnpm test:run`: passed with `66 passed | 1 skipped` test files and `965 passed | 22 skipped` tests.
  - `git diff --check`: passed.

### Implementation evidence

Changed paths in this slice:

- `src/cli/governance.ts`
  - `resolveExplainContextOptions()` now rejects `options.conversationId` without `options.conversationType` using `Conversation type is required when --conversation is provided` before resolving a stored/latest turn.
- `tests/integration/cli-main.test.ts`
  - adds `spawns why conversation-only validation errors without using the latest turn or mutating data`;
  - seeds a valid latest stored turn/context trace containing secret-like data, then calls `why --conversation <sensitive-id>` without `--type`;
  - asserts non-zero exit, empty stdout, concise redacted stderr, no leaked secret/platform/latest-turn/message/memory text, no stack paths, no DB mutation, and clean SQLite FKs.
- `docs/operations.md`
  - documents that `why --conversation` requires explicit `--type private|group` and fails before latest-turn resolution or context rebuild when missing.
- `docs/long-term-development-direction-review.md`
  - updates the active evidence baseline to include this R7 spawned CLI validation slice.
- `docs/loop-state-recovery.md`
  - records this checkpoint and next step.

### Evidence-backed behavior

- `why --conversation <conversation-id>` without `--type` is treated as an invalid incomplete conversation rebuild request.
- The command fails before resolving or displaying the latest stored turn.
- The command fails before ContextBuilder rebuild dispatch.
- Failure stdout is empty and stderr is concise/redacted.
- Sensitive conversation IDs, seeded API-key-like values, seeded QQ/platform-like values, unrelated latest-turn IDs, latest message text, and latest memory titles are not displayed.
- The failure path does not mutate canonical users, raw events, chat messages, agent turns, context traces, audit rows, or memory rows, and SQLite FKs remain clean.

### Remaining risks / gaps

- This is deterministic spawned CLI evidence, not a web UI or live operator session.

  - `pnpm lint`: passed with the same 29 existing `@typescript-eslint/no-non-null-assertion` warnings and 0 errors.
